sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Danny Deep Appointed President of General Dynamics
feed.summary: Danny Deep, a veteran with over 20 years at General Dynamics, will assume the role of president on December 3, 2025, with a base salary of $1.3 million and various incentives. General Dynamics stock (GD) currently holds a Buy rating with a $380.00 price target from analysts, and TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates GD as Outperform due to its strong financials and robust order backlog, though it notes risks from high valuation. The company operates in the aerospace and defense industry, offering a wide range of products and services.

feed.title: SPY ETF Daily Update, 12/5/2025
feed.summary: The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (SPY) was up 0.19% on Friday, marking its fourth consecutive day of gains, driven by hopes of a Federal Reserve interest rate cut. Consumer Discretionary, Technology, and Communication Services sectors performed well within SPY's holdings, while Healthcare, Energy, and Utilities declined. The primary catalyst next week will be the FOMC meeting and interest rate decision, and SPY currently holds a "Moderate Buy" rating with a 15.42% upside potential based on analyst consensus.

feed.title: Fermi America secures power deal with Xcel Energy subsidiary for AI campus By Investing.com
feed.summary: Fermi America has secured an Electric Service Agreement with Southwestern Public Service Company (SPS), an Xcel Energy subsidiary, to provide up to 200 megawatts of electrical capacity for its Project Matador Campus in Amarillo, Texas. This campus is being developed for large-scale artificial intelligence and computing operations. The power deal is a key component of Fermi's plans for a next-generation private electric grid, which will integrate various power sources.

feed.title: Eversource Energy Elects W. Robert Mudge to Board
feed.summary: Eversource Energy announced the election of W. Robert Mudge to its Board of Trustees, effective January 1, 2026. Mudge, with extensive experience in network infrastructure and corporate governance, will also serve on the Audit and Finance and Risk Management Committees, aiming to enhance the company's energy system modernization and reliability. Eversource (ES) currently holds a "Buy" rating from analysts with a $75.00 price target, though TipRanks’ AI Analyst, Spark, rates ES as Neutral due to financial risks offsetting strong earnings and attractive valuation.

feed.title: Viatris Holds Annual Shareholder Meeting on December 5
feed.summary: Viatris Inc. (VTRS) held its annual shareholder meeting on December 5, 2025, where shareholders elected thirteen directors, approved executive compensation for 2024, and ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as the accounting firm for 2025. TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates VTRS as Neutral, citing mixed signals from positive technical momentum and strategic initiatives against financial performance challenges and valuation concerns. The article also notes that the most recent analyst rating on VTRS stock is a Hold with a $12.00 price target.

feed.title: Deere CIO to retire after 28 years with the company
feed.summary: Raj Kalathur, CIO at Deere & Co. and president of John Deere Financial, will retire on January 31, 2026, after 28 years with the company. During his tenure, Kalathur spearheaded the adoption of AI, automation, and agile IT operating models, significantly impacting the company's technology and financial services. John Deere has seen massive technological advancements in the agricultural industry, including precision agriculture and autonomous vehicles, with Kalathur playing a key role in these innovations.

feed.title: Trimble Announces New Credit Agreement and Stock Buyback
feed.summary: Trimble Inc. (TRMB) announced a new $1.25 billion revolving loan facility with Bank of America on December 4, 2025, replacing a previous agreement. Concurrently, the company's Board authorized a new stock repurchase program of up to $1 billion on December 3, 2025, which supersedes the prior program. Spark, TipRanks' AI Analyst, rated TRMB as "Outperform" due to its strong earnings and technical indicators, despite concerns about profitability and valuation.

feed.title: Viatris shareholders elect directors and approve proposals at annual meeting
feed.summary: Viatris Inc. shareholders have elected thirteen director nominees, approved executive compensation on an advisory basis, and ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as independent auditor for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2025. These decisions were made at the company's 2025 annual meeting, as reported in an SEC filing and press release. Separately, Viatris also announced strong Q3 2025 earnings, surpassing analyst expectations for both EPS and revenue, though the stock experienced a decline due to other market factors.

feed.title: Vertex Pharma executive chairman sells $28.6m in stock
feed.summary: Jeffrey M. Leiden, Executive Chairman of Vertex Pharmaceuticals Inc, sold $28.6 million in company stock on December 3, 2025. These sales were part of a pre-arranged trading plan, with stock prices ranging from $440.40 to $455.22 per share. Despite the sales, Leiden also exercised options to acquire 63,782 shares and directly owns 24,026 shares, while Vertex Pharmaceuticals shows strong financial health and has seen its shares gain 13.6% year-to-date.

feed.title: VICI Properties (NYSE: VICI) files S-4 for Golden real estate deal with stock-for-stock merger
feed.summary: VICI Properties has filed a Form S-4 to register common stock for a deal to acquire seven Golden Entertainment real estate properties and related assets. Golden shareholders will receive 0.902 shares of VICI common stock per Golden share, along with a $2.75 cash dividend per share from a separate sale of Golden's operating company to an affiliate of its CEO. The transaction is subject to Golden shareholder approval, regulatory clearances, and the effectiveness of the S-4 registration.

feed.title: Transdigm group director Stein sells shares worth $23.8m
feed.summary: Kevin M Stein, a director at TransDigm Group INC, sold 17,526 shares totaling approximately $23.8 million, while simultaneously acquiring the same number of shares through stock option exercises. This transaction comes as TransDigm recently reported strong financial results for Q4 2025, beating analyst expectations for EPS and revenue. Analysts have issued mixed but generally positive sentiments, with UBS raising its price target and Stifel lowering its target while maintaining a Buy rating.

feed.title: Allegion PLC Declares Quarterly Dividend of $0.51 (NYSE:ALLE)
feed.summary: Allegion PLC (NYSE:ALLE) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.51 per share, to be paid on December 31st to shareholders of record on December 16th. This marks the 11th consecutive year the company has increased its dividend, which is well-covered by earnings with a payout ratio of 24.3%. Allegion recently reported strong Q3 earnings, beating analyst expectations, and provided positive FY2025 guidance.

feed.title: Essex Property Trust declares $2.57 quarterly dividend
feed.summary: Essex Property Trust, Inc. (NYSE:ESS) has declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $2.57 per common share, representing a 3.99% yield. The REIT has a history of 32 consecutive years of dividend payments and specializes in multifamily residential properties on the West Coast. Recent developments include strong third-quarter earnings and the pricing of a $350 million public offering of senior notes due in 2036.

feed.title: The Hershey Company (NYSE: HSY) revises By-laws on Board leadership roles
feed.summary: The Hershey Company's Board of Directors amended and restated its By-laws, effective December 5, 2025, to enhance corporate governance regarding Board leadership and succession. Key changes include removing specific language about Michele Buck's eligibility for Chairman, eliminating the Lead Independent Director role, and clarifying protocols for presiding over meetings during absences or emergencies. These revisions align the By-laws with existing requirements for the Chairman to be an independent director and establish procedures for critical situations.

feed.title: COOPER COMPANIES, INC. SEC 10-K Report
feed.summary: The Cooper Companies, Inc. has released its annual Form 10-K report, detailing financial performance including net sales of $4,092.4 million and a net income of $374.9 million, alongside strategic initiatives such as recent acquisitions and capital management. The report also highlights future outlooks focused on market penetration and product development, while addressing significant challenges and risks like market volatility, international operations, and supply chain disruptions.

feed.title: KeyCorp Hit By Major Activist Push As HoldCo Demands CEO Ouster, Bigger Buybacks And Possible Sale To PNC Or Wells Fargo
feed.summary: HoldCo Asset Management is pressuring KeyCorp to replace its CEO, stop acquisitions, accelerate share buybacks, and consider a sale to a larger bank like PNC or Wells Fargo. The activist investor, which owns about 7.5 million KeyCorp shares, warned it may pursue a proxy fight if the board does not comply with its demands. HoldCo criticized KeyCorp's past strategic decisions, including its pre-2023 interest-rate positioning and capital management, which it claims led to balance-sheet stress and shareholder dilution.

feed.title: Church & Dwight Co. Amends Board Tenure Guidelines
feed.summary: Church & Dwight Co. has removed tenure limits for its Board members, while the retirement age remains at 75. This change, which aligns the company with practices of its S&P 500 peers, aims to balance Board refreshment with continuity. The adjustment was made on December 4, 2025, according to an SEC filing.

feed.title: FDA panel votes against J&J’s novel shunt implant for heart failure
feed.summary: A U.S. FDA advisory panel has unanimously voted against the risk-benefit profile of Johnson & Johnson’s V-Wave Ventura minimally invasive interatrial shunt for heart failure. Despite the device showing safety in the Relieve-HF pivotal trial, it failed to demonstrate significant clinical benefits compared to a control group, particularly among patients with preserved ejection fraction. Panel members called for more rigorous data and clearer evidence of effectiveness for the novel implant.

feed.title: General Mills, Inc. (GIS): A Bull Case Theory
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on General Mills, Inc. (GIS), highlighting its compelling contrarian value opportunity despite trading below its historical peak. The investment case is supported by stable profitability, strong cash generation, a portfolio of iconic brands, and growth catalysts like its pet food segment and cost optimization efforts. Analysts suggest a BUY rating with potential for 25-35% total returns over 2-3 years, and even 40-50% in a bull scenario.

feed.title: Xcel Energy (NASDAQ: XEL) signs ESA to deliver 86–200 MW to Fermi AI site
feed.summary: Fermi America signed an Electric Service Agreement with Southwestern Public Service Company, a subsidiary of Xcel Energy (XEL), to secure up to 200 MW of electrical capacity for its 11 GW Project Matador Campus in Amarillo. Initial delivery of 86 MW will begin in January 2026, ramping up to 200 MW over time via SPS's 115-kilovolt transmission system to support large-scale AI and computing operations. This agreement aligns with Xcel Energy's strategy of expanding load and long-term power commitments, particularly in the growing Texas Panhandle market.

feed.title: Fermi America Executes Electric Service Agreement with Southwestern Public Service Company, a Subsidiary of Xcel Energy, Securing Up to 200 MW of Power
feed.summary: Fermi America has signed a definitive Electric Service Agreement (ESA) with Southwestern Public Service Company (SPS), an Xcel Energy subsidiary, to supply up to 200 megawatts (MW) of electrical capacity to Fermi's 11 GW Project Matador Campus. This partnership enables Fermi America to secure reliable and cost-effective power, supporting its large-scale artificial intelligence and computing operations in Amarillo, Texas. Xcel Energy's SPS will commence delivery of 86 MW in January 2026, gradually scaling up to 200 MW.

feed.title: ConocoPhillips (COP) Is Up 6.9% After Potential Citgo Auction Windfall News Has The Bull Case Changed?
feed.summary: ConocoPhillips (COP) has seen a 6.9% increase in its stock price following news of a potential windfall from the Citgo auction. This article examines how these proceeds could boost its financial flexibility, focusing on its free cash flow and capital allocation strategies. While the additional cash is appealing, the company's long-term reliance on large, capital-intensive projects remains a significant factor in its investment narrative.

feed.title: Will Thermo Fisher's Asia Bioprocessing Tri-Hub Expansion Reshape Thermo Fisher Scientific's (TMO) Narrative?
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific is expanding its bioprocessing capabilities across Asia with a new Bioprocess Design Center in Hyderabad and enlarged facilities in Incheon and Singapore. This "tri-hub" network aims to support faster biologics and cell/gene therapy manufacturing, potentially deepening the company's role as an end-to-end partner in Asia's biopharmaceutical supply chain. The expansion aligns with Thermo Fisher's strategy to invest in capacity and capabilities, though it comes amidst challenges like stabilizing instruments demand and policy uncertainty in China.

feed.title: Vertex Pharmaceuticals Insider Sold Shares Worth $2,024,955, According to a Recent SEC Filing
feed.summary: An insider at Vertex Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:VRTX) recently sold shares valued at $2,024,955, as disclosed in a new SEC filing. This transaction was reported on December 5, 2025, at 04:27 pm EST. The article also mentions other recent news for Vertex Pharmaceuticals, including analyst rating adjustments.

feed.title: Evergy Issues $300M First Mortgage Bonds
feed.summary: Evergy Kansas Central issued $300 million in First Mortgage Bonds at 5.25% due 2035 on December 5, 2025. This new issuance adds to $300 million in bonds issued in March 2025 and was underwritten by BNY Mellon, Regions, TD Securities, and Wells Fargo. The bonds were registered under the Securities Act of 1933.

feed.title: DTE Energy Co Announces 2025-2026 Earnings Guidance and Investment Plans
feed.summary: DTE Energy Co has released its 2025-2026 operating earnings guidance and capital investment plans, increasing its 5-year capital plan by $6.5 billion due to data center agreements. The company targets an annual operating EPS growth of 6%-8% through 2030, emphasizing enhanced customer affordability, reliability, and investments in cleaner generation and infrastructure.

feed.title: Bill pay assistance programs available for neighbors experiencing Dominion Energy price hikes
feed.summary: Dominion Energy customers will face an average increase of $11 in their monthly bills starting in January due to rising infrastructure costs. The company offers several assistance programs, including Energy Share which provides up to $600 for those experiencing financial crises, and budget billing to help manage consistent payments. Additionally, customers can reduce costs by making small changes to their energy usage habits like adjusting thermostats and unplugging devices.

feed.title: BlackRock, Inc. (BLK): A Bull Case Thesis
feed.summary: This article summarizes a bullish thesis on BlackRock, Inc. (BLK) by Rigatoni Capital, highlighting its record AUM of $13.5 trillion and impressive 25% year-over-year revenue growth. BlackRock's diversified exposure across various business lines and strategic management position it for continued growth and significant shareholder value, making it an attractive option for long-term investors.

feed.title: Kenworth offers Paccar TX-12 PRO, TX-18 PRO automated transmissions for mixer applications
feed.summary: Kenworth has expanded the availability of its Paccar TX-12 PRO and TX-18 PRO automated transmissions to T880 and W990 models equipped with Paccar engines and a rear engine power take-off (REPTO). This allows concrete mixer and other REPTO-dependent vocational customers to spec these transmissions on Class 8 trucks. Both transmissions integrate seamlessly with Paccar’s MX-11 and MX-13 engines to maximize uptime and offer features like an Extreme Duty clutch and Rock Free mode.

feed.title: Partial shutdown of Eastern WA paper plant will cut 200 jobs
feed.summary: Packaging Corporation of America (PCA) announced a partial shutdown of its Wallula containerboard plant, resulting in 200 layoffs and a nearly 50% reduction in production capacity. The company cites high operating costs, particularly for wood fiber and purchased power, as the reason for making the mill uncompetitive. The shutdown, which involves closing the No. 2 paper machine and kraft pulping facilities, is projected to be completed by the end of Q1 2026.

feed.title: GSA, Palo Alto Networks Launch OneGov Deal
feed.summary: Palo Alto Networks has partnered with the General Services Administration (GSA) to offer federal agencies discounted rates on its cybersecurity services through a new OneGov agreement. This initiative provides exclusive pricing on AI, cloud, and software defense tools until January 2028, aiming to make securing federal networks more cost-effective and efficient. The agreement includes significant discounts on Prisma AIRS Runtime Security, VM-Series software firewalls, and Code to Cloud solutions, supporting agencies' zero trust and IT modernization efforts.

feed.title: Fastenal and National FFA Organization Launch Corporate Partnership
feed.summary: Fastenal Company announced a new corporate partnership with the National FFA Organization, deepening its commitment to agricultural education and youth development. This partnership will provide targeted support to FFA members and chapters through grants, scholarships, and programs like "Give the Gift of Blue," promoting leadership, personal growth, and career success in agriculture. Fastenal's CEO, Dan Florness, a former FFA participant, emphasized the importance of supporting the next generation of leaders in and outside of agriculture.

feed.title: Fastenal and National FFA Organization Launch Corporate Partnership – Company Announcement
feed.summary: Fastenal Company announced a new corporate partnership with the National FFA Organization, deepening its support for agricultural education and youth development. This partnership includes advancing agricultural education through grants, investing in students via the Fastenal Innovators Scholarship, and fostering belonging with the 'Give the Gift of Blue' program. Fastenal's CEO, Dan Florness, a former FFA participant, expressed pride in supporting the organization's efforts to develop future leaders.

feed.title: Cincinnati financial director Wilkins buys $22927 in stock
feed.summary: Edward Scott Wilkins, a director at Cincinnati Financial Corp (NASDAQ:CINF), recently purchased 140 shares valued at $22,927. This transaction occurred on December 3, 2025, with the stock trading near $162.68, and InvestingPro analysis suggests the company is currently undervalued. Cincinnati Financial boasts a strong financial health score, a consistent dividend payment history, and recently reported strong Q3 2025 earnings.

feed.title: Dollar Tree Stock Rises: What’s Next?
feed.summary: Dollar Tree Inc. (DLTR) saw an 8.04% increase in its stock price due to strong third-quarter earnings, leading a positive market sentiment. The company raised its full-year sales and earnings per share estimations, prompting financial institutions like Citi, Truist, and JPMorgan to increase price targets. Dollar Tree's strategic management and optimistic forecasts for the fourth quarter further reinforce its growth narrative.

feed.title: Ulta Beauty Stock Soars: Earnings Beat and Forecast Raise
feed.summary: Ulta Beauty Inc. (NASDAQ: ULTA) stock surged by 14.11% following strong fiscal third-quarter 2025 results, which saw net sales jump 12.9% to $2.9 billion and EPS hit $5.14, beating expectations. The company also raised its 2025 revenue forecast to $12.3 billion, leading to analyst upgrades from JPMorgan and Goldman Sachs. This positive performance and optimistic outlook have boosted investor confidence, despite cautious sentiments regarding broader economic uncertainties.

feed.title: How American Express is Investing in Future Leaders
feed.summary: American Express is investing in the nonprofit sector through its Leadership Academy, a six-month program designed to enhance the skills of senior leaders. The Academy focuses on developing personal, business, and leadership abilities through in-person seminars, online workshops, and collaborative learning. Graduates report increased confidence, innovation, and commitment to addressing key issues in the nonprofit sector.

feed.title: Verisign Reports Total Domain Registrations Worldwide Reached Over 378 Million at the End of 3Q-2025, Leaping by More Than 16 Million From a Year Ago
feed.summary: Verisign's latest 3Q-2025 Domain Name Industry Brief indicates that total worldwide domain registrations hit 378.5 million, marking a 4.5% increase year-over-year. While .com registrations grew to 159.4 million, .net saw a decline, and new gTLDs demonstrated significant growth with a 21% leap. CcTLDs also expanded, reaching 144.8 million registrations.

feed.title: Holdco Asset Management Urges KeyCorp to Adopt No-Acquisition Policy and Dismiss CEO Christopher Gorman
feed.summary: Holdco Asset Management is pressuring KeyCorp to adopt a no-acquisition policy, use excess capital for share repurchases, and dismiss CEO Christopher Gorman, citing his role in the problematic First Niagara acquisition and subsequent shareholder value destruction. Holdco also seeks the removal of lead independent director Alexander Cutler and the establishment of an independent capital allocation committee. The activist investor is considering a proxy contest or advocating for KeyCorp's sale to other major banks.

feed.title: Cincinnati financial director Wilkins buys $22927 in stock
feed.summary: Edward Scott Wilkins, Financial Director of Cincinnati Financial Corp (NASDAQ:CINF), purchased 140 shares for $22,927. The company, which is considered undervalued by InvestingPro, has a strong financial health rating and a history of consistent dividend payments, recently announcing a quarterly cash dividend coinciding with its 75th anniversary.

feed.title: Caterpillar expands in DFW with purchase of new office space
feed.summary: Caterpillar Inc. is expanding its presence in the Dallas-Fort Worth (DFW) area by purchasing a new office building at 901 W. Walnut Hill Lane in Irving, near its existing Williams Square location. This expansion accommodates the company's continued growth in North Texas, where it has been headquartered since 2022. In 2025, Caterpillar and its employees contributed over $1.9 million to more than 300 local Texas organizations, reinforcing its commitment to the community.

feed.title: American Electric Power announces $1 billion debt offering of subordinated debentures By Investing.com
feed.summary: American Electric Power (AEP) has announced a $1 billion public offering of junior subordinated debentures, comprising $400 million of 5.800% Series C and $600 million of 6.050% Series D debentures, both due 2056. This offering will increase the aggregate principal amount of both series to $1.5 billion each, adding to the company's existing $47.89 billion debt load. The move follows recent Q3 2025 earnings, strategic partnerships, and varied analyst ratings.

feed.title: Pepperdine Gets $4.8 Million From HCA Healthcare for New Nursing School
feed.summary: HCA Healthcare has granted Pepperdine University $4.8 million to support the launch of its new School of Nursing in Calabasas. This initiative aims to address the national nursing shortage, with the school having opened this fall with 68 students and offering various nursing programs. Students will gain practical experience through clinical rotations at HCA Healthcare's Los Robles Regional Medical Center.

feed.title: General Dynamics (GD) valuation after new partnerships, record backlog and three straight quarters of double‑digit earnings growth
feed.summary: General Dynamics (GD) is attracting attention due to new partnerships, AI collaborations, three quarters of double-digit earnings growth, and a record defense backlog. The stock is currently trading at $341.70, showing strong year-to-date and five-year shareholder returns. Analysts suggest a fair value of $380, implying further upside, driven by robust order intake and increased global defense spending, though supply chain issues and shifting defense priorities pose potential risks.

feed.title: Applied Materials (AMAT) Is Up 7.8% After Strong AI-Driven Quarter and Upbeat Guidance - What's Changed
feed.summary: Applied Materials (AMAT) recently reported strong quarterly results and optimistic guidance, driven by high demand for AI-enabled advanced memory and logic chips. This performance highlights the company's central role in AI-related chipmaking tools and its operational strengths. While AI demand acts as a key catalyst, potential risks like cyclicality, export restrictions, and customer concentration persist.

feed.title: Hormel wants to make meals easier with Jennie-O, Applegate
feed.summary: Hormel Foods aims to simplify meal preparation by leveraging its Jennie-O and Applegate brands. The company plans to introduce innovative turkey products and expand into chicken categories like quick-prep breakfast and easy dinner options to meet consumer demand for convenient, healthy meals. This strategy is driven by consumer insights indicating a struggle to find such options, contributing to increased retail and foodservice sales for Hormel in Q4 2025.

feed.title: Will my Netflix subscription go up after Warner Bros Discovery deal? What to know in Tennessee
feed.summary: Netflix announced it will acquire Warner Bros. Discovery, including HBO Max and HBO, in a deal valued at approximately $82.7 billion. This acquisition brings extensive new content like Harry Potter and Game of Thrones to Netflix, aiming to attract more subscribers and generate revenue. While a bundle offering might lower costs for some, experts suggest overall Netflix subscription prices could rise due to reduced competition.

feed.title: Baxter International, Inc. Sued for Securities Law Violations - Investors Should Contact Levi & Korsinsky for More Information - BAX
feed.summary: Levi & Korsinsky, LLP has filed a class action securities lawsuit against Baxter International, Inc. (NYSE: BAX) on behalf of investors who suffered losses due to alleged securities fraud between February 23, 2022, and July 30, 2025. The lawsuit claims Baxter made false statements and concealed systemic defects in its Novum LVP product, leading to widespread malfunctions and patient harm. Investors affected by the alleged fraud have until December 15, 2025, to request appointment as lead plaintiff.

feed.title: HALPER SADEH LLC ENCOURAGES ARCHER-DANIELS-MIDLAND COMPANY SHAREHOLDERS TO CONTACT THE FIRM TO DISCUSS THEIR RIGHTS
feed.summary: Halper Sadeh LLC, an investor rights law firm, is investigating whether Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (NYSE: ADM) officers and directors breached their fiduciary duties to shareholders. The firm encourages long-term shareholders to contact them to discuss potential legal options, which may include corporate governance reforms, the return of funds, or financial incentives. Halper Sadeh LLC offers to handle the action on a contingent fee basis.

feed.title: Thomas Carey Sells 10,214 Shares of Broadridge Financial Solutions (NYSE:BR) Stock
feed.summary: Broadridge Financial Solutions VP Thomas Carey sold 10,214 shares of the company's stock on December 3rd, reducing his stake by 62.89%. The company also declared a quarterly dividend of $0.975 per share, and analysts currently have a "Hold" rating on the stock with a consensus price target of $259.83.
